cases.title: Testing-plan Attempt/audit coverage row: every check-in submission persists CheckInAttempt
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 Attempt/audit coverage block applies (FR-22, FR-29, FR-30, BR-22, BR-23, AC-18, AC-19).
Open class session exists with enrolled students; fixtures support one successful and one deterministic failure path.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins for enrolled student A with valid qrToken within present window.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins for enrolled student B with expired qrToken.
Query check_in_attempts for both student-session pairs and verify row count and outcome fields.
cases.expected: BR-23 satisfied for both paths: every terminal check-in submission persists exactly one CheckInAttempt with structured outcome (Success or rejection code), non-null submittedAt, student and session foreign keys, and token reference.

cases.title: Check-in transaction boundary inserts CheckInAttempt before attendance write on success
preconditions: Real database transaction is enabled for check-in command processing per API design §5.3.
Open session with valid rotating QR token exists; enrolled student has no AttendanceRecord for the session.
Test harness can capture qr_session_token_id and correlation_id on write path.
steps: Submit check-in command with valid qrToken and clientTimestamp within present window.
Query check_in_attempts for outcome Success, qr_session_token_id, submitted_at, and correlation_id.
Query attendance_records for source_attempt_id linkage to the inserted attempt row.
cases.expected: Success path inserts one CheckInAttempt with outcome Success, non-null qr_session_token_id reference, ISO-8601 submitted_at, and correlation metadata before AttendanceRecord is created; DI-10 invariant holds.

cases.title: Failed GPS validation persists optional GPS summary on CheckInAttempt
preconditions: Effective policy for class section requires GPS validation with configured room radius.
Open session exists; enrolled student has no success AttendanceRecord.
Test harness can supply GPS coordinates outside allowed radius with accuracyMeters within threshold.
steps: Submit check-in command with gps payload placing student outside configured radius.
Query check_in_attempts for outcome OutOfRadius, gps_latitude, gps_longitude, gps_accuracy_meters, distance_from_room_meters, and gps_validation_result.
Query attendance_records to confirm no Present/Late row was created.
cases.expected: BR-23 requires optional GPS validation summary on attempt row: OutOfRadius outcome with populated coordinate fields, distance_from_room_meters, gps_validation_result Fail, and submitted_at timestamp; no AttendanceRecord success mutation.

cases.title: SM-04 terminal failure states each persist CheckInAttempt with matching outcome code
preconditions: Integration database seeded with fixtures for Scheduled, Closed, and Open sessions.
Enrolled students available for each failure path; validation order short-circuit paths individually reachable.
steps: Drive check-in against Scheduled session; capture SessionNotOpen attempt.
Drive check-in against Closed session; capture SessionClosed attempt.
Drive check-in with expired token on Open session; capture ExpiredQr attempt.
For each path, verify AttendanceRecord remains absent or unchanged.
cases.expected: Each SM-04 business-failure terminal state persists one CheckInAttempt with structured outcome code matching the short-circuit reason and non-null submitted_at; BR-23 exception clause (none) means zero submissions skip attempt logging.

cases.title: Concurrent distinct submissions each insert separate CheckInAttempt rows
preconditions: Open session with valid rotating QR tokens exists.
Two enrolled students A and B have no success AttendanceRecord for the session.
Test harness can fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins requests with distinct Idempotency-Key values.
steps: Submit concurrent POST /v1/check-ins for student A and student B with valid tokens.
Query check_in_attempts filtered by class_session_id and count rows per student.
Verify each terminal outcome has its own attempt row with submitted_at and token reference.
cases.expected: Each concurrent submission persists its own CheckInAttempt with terminal outcome and timestamp per BR-23; no attempt row is dropped or merged under peak load.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins 200 success envelope correlates with persisted Success CheckInAttempt
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists in test environment.
Authenticated enrolled student bearer token is available.
Request includes Idempotency-Key and X-Request-Id headers.
steps: Call POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization Bearer token, valid qrToken, clientTimestamp, and Idempotency-Key.
Inspect HTTP 200 response envelope data.outcome Success and meta.requestId.
Query persisted CheckInAttempt by correlation_id or requestId and compare outcome, submitted_at, and qr_session_token_id.
cases.expected: API returns HTTP 200 with data.outcome Success; persisted CheckInAttempt.outcome equals Success with matching correlation_id, non-null submitted_at, and token reference per BR-23 and API §5.3 step 4.

cases.title: POST /v1/check-ins 422 error.code maps one-to-one to persisted CheckInAttempt outcome
preconditions: Open class session with rotating QR token exists in test environment.
Authenticated enrolled student bearer token is available.
Test clock can advance beyond token expiresAt.
steps: Advance clock past qr.expiresAt for the current session token.
Call POST /v1/check-ins with Authorization Bearer token, expired qrToken, clientTimestamp, and Idempotency-Key.
Inspect HTTP 422 response envelope error.code and meta.requestId.
Query persisted CheckInAttempt for the request correlation id and compare outcome field.
cases.expected: API returns 422 with error.code ExpiredQr; persisted CheckInAttempt.outcome equals ExpiredQr with non-null submitted_at and token reference per BR-23 100% failed-attempt reason coverage.

cases.title: WF-03 primary workflow logs success CheckInAttempt after auth and check-in submission
preconditions: Lecturer has opened session; rotating valid QR token is displayed.
Unauthenticated enrolled student account exists with known credentials.
Student has no AttendanceRecord for the session.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/auth/login and receives accessToken with Student role.
Student calls POST /v1/check-ins with Bearer token, valid qrToken, and clientTimestamp within present window.
Query check_in_attempts and attendance_records for the student-session pair.
cases.expected: WF-03 happy path persists one CheckInAttempt with outcome Success, token reference, submitted_at, and correlation metadata per BR-23; AttendanceRecord links to the attempt row with Present or Late status.

cases.title: Documented failure outcome matrix achieves 100% CheckInAttempt persistence with structured reason codes
preconditions: Test matrix covers canonical outcomes: ExpiredQr, SessionNotOpen, SessionClosed, NotEnrolled, DuplicateCheckIn, GpsRequired, GpsDisabled, OutOfRadius, LowAccuracy.
Each row has a deterministic fixture to reach that terminal outcome without bypassing validation order.
steps: For each documented failure outcome code, trigger POST /v1/check-ins and capture HTTP status and error.code.
Query check_in_attempts filtered by studentUserId and classSessionId for each triggered failure.
Aggregate results against NFR-13 minimum evidence: 100% failed attempts include structured reason code.
cases.expected: Every matrix failure path returns documented error.code and persists exactly one CheckInAttempt with the same structured outcome, non-null submitted_at, and token reference; zero terminal failures lack a persisted attempt row per BR-23.

cases.title: Rule-order short-circuit BR-01 through BR-10 still logs attempt with first-failure reason code
preconditions: Fixtures exist where multiple downstream rules would fail if session were Open: expired token, not enrolled, duplicate success, GPS outside radius.
Scheduled session exists to test BR-01 short-circuit before BR-03, BR-06, BR-07.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with expired token against Scheduled session; assert error.code SessionNotOpen and query CheckInAttempt outcome.
Open session; submit as unenrolled student with valid token; assert NotEnrolled attempt logged.
Submit as already-checked-in student with GPS outside radius; assert DuplicateCheckIn not OutOfRadius in attempt outcome.
Verify each submission has exactly one attempt row regardless of which rule short-circuited.
cases.expected: Validation order per API §5.1 and business rules §11: each short-circuited submission persists CheckInAttempt with the first-failure structured reason code; BR-23 holds that logging is unconditional with no exception path.

cases.title: Idempotency replay and duplicate submission both produce auditable CheckInAttempt rows
preconditions: Student has completed one successful POST /v1/check-ins for an Open session with Idempotency-Key K1.
Session remains Open with freshly rotated valid QR token.
Idempotency store retains accepted command for (K1, actor, token) within replay window.
steps: Replay POST /v1/check-ins with identical Idempotency-Key K1 and same qrToken; inspect response and query attempt rows.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with new Idempotency-Key K2 and fresh valid qrToken for same student-session.
Query check_in_attempts for student-session pair and count rows by outcome.
cases.expected: Idempotency replay of K1 returns prior success summary; new-key duplicate returns 409 DuplicateCheckIn and persists failed CheckInAttempt with structured outcome; BR-07 one-success-per-session enforced while BR-23 logs every distinct submission attempt.

cases.title: Student export denied and cross-scope Lecturer cannot read attempt metadata from non-assigned roster
preconditions: Open session exists in section A assigned to Lecturer-A with student who has failed ExpiredQr CheckInAttempt.
Lecturer-B is authenticated but not assigned to section A.
Student account is authenticated with enrollment in section A.
BR-18 scopes export to actor role; BR-19 denies unauthorized report access.
steps: Student calls POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting section A.
Assert HTTP 403 Forbidden without partial export data leakage.
Lecturer-B calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for section A session.
Assert HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden without Student A attempt details.
Lecturer-A (control) calls same roster endpoint and inspects rejectedAttempts and latestAttemptOutcome.
cases.expected: Student institution-wide export denied per BR-18; cross-scope Lecturer denied roster read per BR-19 without disclosing CheckInAttempt metadata; assigned Lecturer control sees scoped rejectedAttempts and latestAttemptOutcome derived from persisted attempts.

cases.title: UI journey maps API failure reason to CheckInResultScreen and lecturer roster attempt badge
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile-web browser at PG-02 check-in page.
Open session exists; test can supply an expired QR token via deep link query parameter.
Lecturer is logged in on desktop at PG-06 live roster for the same session.
steps: Student navigates to /check-in?token=<expired> and submits check-in.
Observe CheckInResultScreen renders failure-expired-qr danger alert with localized copy and retry CTA.
Verify via API or harness that a CheckInAttempt with outcome ExpiredQr was persisted for the submission.
Lecturer refreshes PG-06 roster and inspects rejectedAttempts count and student row latestAttemptOutcome badge.
cases.expected: Student UI maps API ExpiredQr to failure-expired-qr state; each visible failure corresponds to one persisted CheckInAttempt per BR-23; lecturer roster shows incremented rejectedAttempts and latestAttemptOutcome ExpiredQr without exposing other students' attempt details.
